title: Abolish the ATF Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill would shut down the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, which is also called the ATF. The ATF is a government agency that makes sure people follow laws about alcohol, tobacco, guns, and explosives. If this bill becomes law, the ATF would no longer exist. The bill does not say what would happen to the ATF's workers or who would take over their job of enforcing these laws.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Abolish the ATF Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Abolishment of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives
sections.text: The Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (a federal government agency that enforces laws about alcohol, tobacco, guns, and explosives) is eliminated and will no longer exist.
